schema_notes: concept-level critical-risk findings plus CVE crosswalk checks; strict all-of must-hit

must_hit_critical_risks.concept: plaintext API_KEY baked into Dockerfile ENV
must_hit_critical_risks.rationale: A static credential committed in the image is exfiltrated to anyone who pulls or inspects the image; must be moved to runtime secret injection.

must_hit_critical_risks.where: server.py line 7-8: blob = request.data; obj = pickle.loads(blob) inside POST /load
must_hit_critical_risks.rationale: Calling pickle.loads on untrusted bytes is direct remote code execution; the /load endpoint accepts arbitrary POST bodies.
Werkzeug's interactive debugger gives RCE to any reachable client; combined with 0.0.0.0 binding this is a remotely exploitable shell.

must_hit_critical_risks.concept: container runs as root on python:latest with no USER directive
must_hit_critical_risks.where: Dockerfile line 1 (FROM python:latest) and absence of any USER directive
must_hit_critical_risks.rationale: The image inherits the floating python:latest tag and never drops privileges, so a process compromise becomes a root-in-container compromise on an unpredictable base.

must_hit_critical_risks.concept: requests==2.20.0 affected by CVE-2018-18074 (Authorization header leak on redirect)
must_hit_critical_risks.where: requirements.txt line 2: requests==2.20.0; vuln_advisories.json advisory CVE-2018-18074 (fixed_version 2.20.1)
must_hit_critical_risks.rationale: The pinned requirement matches an explicit advisory in vuln_advisories.json with a known fix; missing this means the auditor did not actually use the supplied feed.

ground_truth.must_hit_match_policy: All 5 risks must be hit. For each risk, the executor's audit.md must reference the concept clearly enough that any of match_keywords_any (case-insensitive) matches the title or evidence/body of at least one finding. Strict 5/5; missing even one risk → CP §5 line scores 0.
docker_remediation_anchors: pin base image by version or digest
copy requirements before app code for cacheable reproducible installs
create and switch to a non-root USER
remove secrets from Dockerfile and inject via runtime secret management

inapplicable_advisories.reason_inapplicable: django is not in this bundle (requirements.txt has flask, requests, pyyaml, jinja2 only). The advisory feed lists it but the package is not used; flagging it as a real finding is a false positive.

Affects Windows builds of openssl 3.0.0–3.0.7 only. The Dockerfile uses python:latest (Linux-based); this CVE does not apply to a Linux container. Flagging is a false positive unless the audit explicitly notes the platform mismatch.
lxml is not in this bundle's requirements.txt. The advisory feed lists it but the package is not installed; flagging is a false positive.

ground_truth.inapplicable_advisories_note: Note: 3 misleading entries exist in vuln_advisories.json. A competent audit must cross-reference each advisory against the bundle's actual requirements.txt + Dockerfile + server.py before flagging. Listing any of these 3 CVEs as a real finding (without explicitly noting it does NOT apply) → §6 cap 0.50.
